---
name: anime-bubble-design
description: Use this skill when the user asks for a UI redesign, frontend styling, or component polish in this project. Prioritize a distinctive anime bubble aesthetic and avoid generic frontend layouts.
applyTo: src/**/*.{ts,tsx,css}
---

This repository is a small, static React + TypeScript + Vite app. When designing or refining UI here, do the following:

- Commit fully to a cohesive visual theme. For this project, prefer soft pastel colors, bubble shapes, glowing accents, and frosted glass surfaces.
- Use CSS variables for palette consistency and Tailwind CSS utility classes where appropriate.
- Add atmosphere with layered backgrounds, gradients, subtle blur, and floating bubble elements.
- Use rounded layouts: cards, buttons, and squares should feel soft, pill-like, and playful.
- Enhance motion with CSS-based animations and transitions. Focus on a few meaningful effects rather than many weak ones.
- Prefer expressive typography and avoid default system or overly generic UI aesthetics. Use stylish font combinations that feel energetic and whimsical.
- Preserve application functionality. Do not change game logic or data flow when the user explicitly asks for a visual redesign.

Avoid:

- plain gray/white admin-style interfaces
- generic purple gradients or cookie-cutter layouts without character
- excessive reliance on utility classes that ignore the overall mood
- introducing backend complexity or new architectural layers for a purely visual update
